title: Authentication, Passwords, Sessions, Cookies, and JOSE · Type-System Trace: Authentication Principal Audit
summary: The server stores weak password material, keeps session identifiers across privilege changes, decodes JWTs without validating them, accepts arbitrary algorithms, and treats possession as authorization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts authentication principal audit increment in credential verification and principal-construction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct the smallest principal after full credential validation and record redacted success, failure class, rotation, revocation, and recovery evidence.
Bound JWKS retrieval, issuer mapping, cache lifetime, refresh, key rotation, key identifiers, algorithm confusion, stale behavior, and outage response.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Authentication Principal Audit · Theory Authentication Principal Au…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6843 in a administrative API crossing two identity and network trust zones. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate bounded token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Construct the smallest principal after full credential validation and record redacted success, failure class, rotation, revocation, and recovery evidence.
